figures.caption: Figure 4: Numerical simulations of the discrete transformer dynamics with Sigmoid activation, for N = 64 tokens over L = 150 layers (T = 5.0), dimensions d ∈ {5, 64, 128}, bias levels σ ∈ {0.0, 0.1}, and attention scaling 1, 0, and −1. The panels show the pairwise cosine similarities and the interaction energy Eβ(Xℓ).

Figure 5: Numerical simulations of the discrete transformer dynamics with Tanh activation, for N = 64 tokens over L = 150 layers (T = 5.0), dimensions d ∈ {5, 64, 128}, bias levels σ ∈ {0.0, 0.1}, and attention scaling 1, 0, and −1. The panels show the pairwise cosine similarities and the interaction energy Eβ(Xℓ). 54

Figure 6: Numerical simulations of the discrete transformer dynamics without MLPs, for N = 64 tokens over L = 150 layers (T = 5.0), dimensions d ∈ {5, 64, 128}, bias levels σ ∈ {0.0, 0.1}, and attention scaling 1, 0, and −1. The panels show the pairwise cosine similarities and the interaction energy Eβ(Xℓ).
Table 1: Computed kernel values, top Lyapunov exponent (λ1), and stability parameter (λ̄) for various activation functions, dimensions d, and bias standard deviations σ.

Figure 2: Numerical simulations of the discrete transformer dynamics with ReLU activation, for N = 64 tokens over L = 150 layers (T = 5.0), dimensions d ∈ {5, 64, 128}, bias levels σ ∈ {0.0, 0.1}, and attention scaling 1, 0, and −1. The panels show the pairwise cosine similarities and the interaction energy Eβ(Xℓ). 51

Figure 1: Noise-induced synchronization without attention. Evolution of the first two components of N = 64 tokens on S3 (d = 4) over L = 2000 layers (T = 20), with attention disabled and MLP width m = 2048 and variance parameters σ2 U = σ2 W = 0. For both ReLU and Tanh activations, an initially near-uniform token configuration evolves into localized clusters, illustrating the contractive effect of the common MLP noise discussed in Section 1.3. We note that under the given choice of parameters, the activation ϕ = tanh yields an a.s. odd random field, violating Assumption 1 and resulting in a 2-point random attractor.

Figure 3: Numerical simulations of the discrete transformer dynamics with SiLU activation, for N = 64 tokens over L = 150 layers (T = 5.0), dimensions d ∈ {5, 64, 128}, bias levels σ ∈ {0.0, 0.1}, and attention scaling 1, 0, and −1. The panels show the pairwise cosine similarities and the interaction energy Eβ(Xℓ). 52
